channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Atletico Madrid Defeat Barcelona in Champions League Quarter-Final First Leg
item.description: Ah, so Barcelona chop beating for Camp Nou! Atletico Madrid beat dem 2-0 for the first leg of their Champions League quarter-final match. Things weren't looking good for Barca, you know? Dem were domi...

item.title: Melbourne Apartment Shooting: Man Dies in Targeted Attack
item.description: A man has died after being fatally shot in a targeted attack inside a Box Hill apartment complex in Melbourne’s east. The incident occurred just before 2:00 AM on Thursday, with residents reporting a ...

item.title: Bombay High Court Cancels Ponda Assembly Bypoll in Goa Over Remaining Term Issue
item.description: On 8th April 2026, the Bombay High Court in Goa quashed the notification for the Ponda assembly bypoll, which was scheduled for 9th April, following a petition challenging the Election Commission of I...

item.title: ODM Secretary-General Sifuna petitions tribunal over attempted ouster
item.description: ODM Secretary-General Edwin Sifuna has gone back to the Political Parties Disputes Tribunal asking for protection against an ongoing push to remove him from his post. He claims that some party members...

item.title: KRA initiates recruitment of new Commissioner General after removal of Humphrey Wattanga
item.description: The Kenya Revenue Authority (KRA) has parted ways with its Commissioner General, Humphrey Wattanga Mulongo, marking the end of his term at the helm of the tax agency. The development was confirmed by ...

item.title: Cardoso demands continued dominance as Sundowns secure 10 straight league wins
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns coach Miguel Cardoso has called for his team to keep extending their impressive winning streak after the Brazilians collected all 30 points from 10 successive victories since January...

item.title: Felista Mugo, Kenya’s First Female MMA Fighter, Set for PFL Africa Debut Against Uganda’s Annet Kiiza
item.description: Felista Mugo is making history as Kenya’s first female Mixed Martial Arts fighter. The 24-year-old from South B in Nairobi is set to step into the big stage at PFL Africa Season 2 this Friday, 10th Ap...

item.title: Parliament rejects proposed payment of overdue media bills totaling Sh826 million
item.description: Kenyan MPs have rejected a proposal that sought to clear outstanding media bills worth Sh826 million owed by different government departments. This decision came during parliamentary discussions about...

item.title: Edmonton man faces 26 charges including human trafficking and bestiality
item.description: A 47-year-old Edmonton man, Jonathan Allard, has been charged with 26 criminal offences related to human trafficking and animal abuse. Alberta Law Enforcement Response Teams (ALERT) allege that Allard...

item.title: Raghav Chadha Shares Reel Suggesting He May Float New Party Amid Growing Rift with AAP
item.description: In a significant development in Indian politics, Aam Aadmi Party Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha has sparked fresh speculation about his future by sharing an Instagram reel on his story. The reel, create...

item.title: Edmonton Auto Experience 2026 features vehicles from economy cars to luxury models at Expo Centre
item.description: The Edmonton Auto Experience returns for its second year, taking place from April 9 to 12, 2026, at the Edmonton EXPO Centre. This automotive event showcases a wide range of vehicles, appealing to all...

item.title: Quebec company faces class-action lawsuit over adulterated maple syrup
item.description: A Quebec-based producer, Érablière Steve Bourdeau, is facing a class-action lawsuit filed in Quebec Superior Court for allegedly selling maple syrup mixed with cane sugar and misrepresenting its origi...

item.title: EFF Echoes Rwanda’s Call for Unity During Genocide Commemoration
item.description: The Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF), a South African opposition party, joined Rwandans and the international community in commemorating the 1994 Genocide against the Tutsi. The party's statement marke...

item.title: Iranian Hackers Target US Infrastructure, Disrupt Oil, Gas, and Water Systems
item.description: Iran-linked hackers have disrupted the operations of U.S. oil, gas, and water companies, causing significant financial and operational losses. The cyberattacks, which began as early as January 2025, t...

item.title: RAF Ibsley control tower to be restored as self-catering holiday accommodation
item.description: The historic RAF Ibsley control tower in Ringwood, Hampshire, which played a notable role during World War Two and featured in the 1941 film 'The First of the Few', is set to be transformed into a hol...

item.title: President Marcos Calls on Filipinos to Embody Bravery and Integrity During Araw ng Kagitingan Commemoration
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. encouraged Filipinos to demonstrate bravery by doing what is right during the commemoration of Araw ng Kagitingan, also known as Day of Valor. The event honors the coura...

item.title: Government extends time limit for victims to appeal against lenient sentences to six months
item.description: The UK government has announced major changes to the existing Unduly Lenient Sentence Scheme, extending the time allowed for victims and bereaved families to challenge sentences they believe are too l...

item.title: Delhi HC Rejects Christian Michel's Release Plea in AgustaWestland Helicopter Scam
item.description: The Delhi High Court has dismissed a petition filed by British arms consultant Christian James Michel, a key figure in the alleged AgustaWestland helicopter scam, seeking his release from prison. Mich...

item.title: DSWD aims to complete ₱5,000 cash aid distribution to tricycle and NCR PUV drivers before May
item.description: The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) announced that it will complete the distribution of the ₱5,000 relief assistance for tricycle drivers and for public utility vehicle (PUV) drive...

item.title: Ongoing Middle East Conflict: Key Updates for April 9, 2026
item.description: As of April 9, 2026, the Middle East conflict continues to intensify, with new reports emerging from across the region. On April 8, 2026, a recent Israeli airstrike in Tyre, Lebanon, left buildings in...
